eventMetadata.context_description: Crystal Palace enters the UEFA Conference League semi-final second leg at Selhurst Park holding a commanding 3-1 aggregate lead after their dominant away win over Shakhtar Donetsk last week, fueling trader consensus at 62.5% for a home victory driven by home advantage, attacking momentum from Ismaïla Sarr and Jean-Philippe Mateta, and seven straight unbeaten home games. Shakhtar's impressive ten-match away unbeaten streak and recent 2-1 league win over Dynamo Kyiv keep them viable at 16%, but centre-back Marlon's injury doubt weakens their defense needing two goals without reply. The 22.5% draw probability reflects Shakhtar's likely aggressive approach clashing with Palace's control.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Strasbourg Alsace enters the UEFA Conference League semifinal second leg at Stade de la Meinau holding a 49% implied probability to win, reflecting trader consensus on their strong home form and urgency to overturn Rayo Vallecano's slender 1-0 first-leg aggregate lead from Alemão's header. Strasbourg dominated possession late in Madrid but lacked clinical finishing amid absences of Joaquín Panichelli (knee), Aaron Anselmino (hamstring), and suspended/injured Valentin Barco, with Guéla Doué still doubtful from illness. Rayo, missing top Conference scorer Álvaro García (hamstring), Luiz Felipe, and Randy Nteka, earned a vital clean sheet through resilient defending, yet their 23.5% away win chance underscores the competitive matchup and draw risk at 26%, as both sides prioritize progression over the 90-minute result.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Strasbourg Alsace face Rayo Vallecano de Madrid in the UEFA Conference League semi-final second leg on May 7 at Stade de la Meinau, following the first leg on April 30 at Estadio de Vallecas, with both clubs nursing key injury concerns amid strong European runs. Strasbourg, eighth in Ligue 1, recently overturned a 2-0 deficit against Mainz to reach the semis but fell 0-1 to Nice in the Coupe de France semi-final on April 22, missing Guela Doué (virus), Valentin Barco (ankle/suspension), Aaron Anselmino (hamstring), and season-ending ACL victim Joaquín Panichelli. Rayo, 11th in La Liga, advanced past AEK Athens on aggregate despite doubts over Randy Nteka (discomfort), Luiz Felipe (thigh), Álvaro García (muscle), and Fran Pérez (back). Home advantage and rest could favor Strasbourg, but squad depth will test trader consensus on aggregate progression.

eventMetadata.context_description: Crystal Palace hosts Shakhtar Donetsk in the UEFA Europa Conference League semi-final second leg on May 7 at Selhurst Park, following the first leg on neutral turf in Krakow on April 30, where Shakhtar's home games are played amid ongoing conflict. Both sides advanced convincingly in quarters—Palace edging Fiorentina 4-2 aggregate despite a 2-1 second-leg loss, Shakhtar dominating AZ Alkmaar 5-2 overall—with recent domestic form bolstering confidence: Shakhtar's 2-1 win at Zorya on April 23 caps three straight victories as Ukrainian Premier League leaders, while mid-table Palace drew West Ham on April 20 after a dramatic 2-1 stoppage-time triumph over Newcastle. Key concerns include Palace's season-ending hamstring injury to striker Eddie Nketiah and doubts over Adam Wharton and Maxence Lacroix, versus Shakhtar midfielder Isaque's recovery from a head knock against AZ; home advantage and Glasner's tactical setup could prove decisive in this evenly poised tie.
